title: Detection of Target Molecules in Mouse Brain Slices Using Peroxidase-Fused Nanobodies

steps.title: 293T cell culture medium Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: 293T cell culture medium Reagent Final concentration Volume DMEM 90% 500 mL FBS 10% 50 mL L-glutamine 2 mM 5 mL MEM non-essential amino acid (100×) 1× 5 mL Penicillin-Streptomycin (100×) 1× 5 mL Total 565 mL Swirl the bottle to mix. The medium can be stored at 4 °C for 3 months.

steps.title: Lipofectamine 3000 solution Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Lipofectamine 3000 solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 1 well) Lipofectamine 3000 Reagent n/a 7.5 μL Opti-MEM n/a 125 μL Total 132.5 μL Mix well by vortexing for 2–3 s. The solution should be prepared before use. Recipes 2, 3, and 4 are part of the sequential steps for preparing transfection complexes.

steps.title: Plasmid DNA with P3000 solution Reagent...
steps.instruction: Plasmid DNA with P3000 solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 1 well) pCAG-GFP1 POD-nAb-WPRE (1.0 μg/μL) 2.5 μg/tube 2.5 μL P3000 reagent 2 μL/μg DNA 5 μL Opti-MEM n/a 125 μL Total 132.5 μL Dilute DNA in Opti-MEM and then add P3000 reagent. Mix well by pipetting. The solution should be prepared before use. Recipes 2, 3, and 4 are part of the sequential steps for preparing transfection complexes.

steps.title: Plasmid DNA–lipid complex Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Plasmid DNA–lipid complex Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 1 well) Lipofectamine 3000 solution 50% 125 μL DNA with P3000 solution 50% 125 μL Total 250 μL Mix by tapping the tube. Incubate for 10–15 min at 20–25 °C. The solution should be prepared before use. Recipes 2, 3, and 4 are part of the sequential steps for preparing transfection complexes.

steps.title: Thimerosal 10% Reagent Final concentration Volume...
steps.instruction: Thimerosal 10% Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 10 mL) Thimerosal 10% 1 g Double-distilled water (ddH 2 O) n/a up to 10 mL Total 10 mL Mix well by vortexing. The solution can be stored at 20–25 °C for 1 year. Caution: Thimerosal is an alkylmercury compound. Avoid inhalation or contact with skin, eyes, and mucous membrane. Handle it inside a fume hood with appropriate protective gear. Critical: NaN 3 as a preservative should be avoided. NaN 3 inactivates the enzymatic activity of POD.

steps.title: PBS Reagent Final concentration Volume (for...
steps.instruction: PBS Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 1 L) NaCl 137 mM 8 g Na 2 HPO 4 ·12H 2 O 8.1 mM 2.9 g KCl 2.7 mM 200 mg KH 2 PO 4 1.5 mM 200 mg ddH 2 O n/a up to 1 L Total 1 L Mix well by stirring. The solution can be stored at 20–25 °C for 6 months.

steps.title: AAV solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 100 μL) AAV2/PHP
steps.instruction: AAV solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 100 μL) AAV2/PHP.eB CAG-EGFP-WPRE 5.0 × 10 10 genome copies/mL n/a PBS n/a 100 μL Total 100 μL Mix well by pipetting. The solution should be prepared before use. Caution: Biosafety should be considered with respect to the precise nature of experiments being conducted. AAV vectors can be handled in BSL-1 facilities in most cases. Critical: The optimal concentration of the AAV-GFP vector should be determined for each experiment.

steps.title: PFA 16% Reagent Final concentration Volume...
steps.instruction: PFA 16% Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 1 L) PFA 16% 160 g 10 N NaOH solution n/a n/a ddH 2 O n/a up to 1 L Total 1 L Add 160 g of PFA in ddH 2 O at 60–70 °C. Dissolve PFA by adding 10 N NaOH solution and stirring. Allow the solution to cool to 20–25 °C and bring the volume to 1 L with ddH 2 O. Filter the solution through filter paper and dispense to 10 mL each. The solution can be stored at -20 °C for 1 year. Caution: PFA is toxic and teratogenic. Avoid inhalation or contact with skin, eyes, and mucous membranes. Handle it inside a fume hood with appropriate protective gear.

0.2 M Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 1 L) NaH 2 PO 4 ·2H 2 O 38 mM 11.8 g Na 2 HPO 4 ·12H 2 O 162 mM 116 g ddH 2 O n/a up to 1 L Total 1 L Mix well by stirring. The solution can be stored at 20–25 °C for 6 months.

steps.title: Fixative solution Reagent Final concentration Volume...
steps.instruction: Fixative solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 40 mL) PFA 16% 4% 10 mL PB (pH 7.

0.2 M 0.1 M 20 mL ddH 2 O n/a up to 40 mL Total 40 mL Filter the solution through filter paper. Mix well by swirling. Use the solution within the same day. Caution: PFA is toxic and teratogenic. Avoid inhalation or contact with skin, eyes, and mucous membranes. Handle it inside a fume hood with appropriate protective gear.

steps.title: Sodium pentobarbital solution Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Sodium pentobarbital solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 20 mL) Pentobarbital sodium salt 50 mg/mL 1 g PBS n/a up to 20 mL Total 20 mL Mix well by stirring. The solution can be stored at 20–25 °C for 1 month.

steps.title: 4% agar in PBS Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: 4% agar in PBS Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 50 mL) Agar 4% 2 g PBS n/a up to 50 mL Total 50 mL Mix well by stirring. Melt agar by heating in a microwave. The solution should be prepared before use.
0.1 M Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 1 L) PB (pH 7.
0.2 M 0.2 M 500 mL ddH 2 O n/a up to 1 L Total 1 L Swirl the bottle to mix. The solution can be stored at 20–25 °C for 3 months.

steps.title: Triton X-100 10% (w/v) Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: Triton X-100 10% (w/v) Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 50 mL) Triton X-100 10% (w/v) 5 g ddH 2 O n/a up to 50 mL Total 50 mL Mix well by stirring. The solution can be stored at 4 °C for 6 months.

steps.title: Sca l eA2 solution Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: Sca l eA2 solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 100 mL) Urea 4 M 24.02 g Glycerol 10% (w/v) 10 g Triton-X100 10% (w/v) 0.1% (w/v) 1 mL ddH 2 O n/a up to 100 mL Total 100 mL Mix well by stirring. The solution can be stored at 4 °C for 1 month.

steps.title: Endogenous peroxidase quenching solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 6
steps.instruction: Endogenous peroxidase quenching solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 6.2 mL) H 2 O 2 31% 1% 200 μL PBS n/a 6 mL Total 6.2 mL Mix well by swirling. The solution should be prepared before use. Caution: H 2 O 2 is corrosive and can cause local tissue damage. Avoid inhalation or contact with skin, eyes, and mucous membranes. Handle it inside a fume hood with appropriate protective gear.

steps.title: Triton X-100 10% (v/v) Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: Triton X-100 10% (v/v) Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 500 mL) Triton X-100 10% (v/v) 50 mL ddH 2 O n/a up to 500 mL Total 500 mL Mix well by stirring. The solution can be stored at 4 °C for 6 months.

steps.title: PBS-X Reagent Final concentration Volume (for...
steps.instruction: PBS-X Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 1 L) Triton X-100 10% (v/v) 0.3% (v/v) 30 mL PBS n/a up to 1 L Total 1 L Swirl the bottle to mix. The solution can be stored at 20–25 °C for 3 months.

steps.title: POD-nAb solution Reagent Final concentration Volume...
steps.instruction: POD-nAb solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 1,200 μL) POD-nAb culture supernatant 20% 600 μL Triton X-100 10% (v/v) 0.3% (v/v) 36 μL Thimerosal 10% 0.02% 2.4 μL PBS n/a up to 1,200 μL Total 1,200 μL Mix well by pipetting. The solution should be prepared before use. Caution: Thimerosal is an alkylmercury compound. Avoid inhalation or contact with skin, eyes, and mucous membranes. Handle it inside a fume hood with appropriate protective gear. Critical: The optimal concentration of POD-nAb culture supernatant should be determined for each experiment.

steps.title: CF tyramide solution Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: CF tyramide solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 1 mL) CF tyramide 2 mM 2 μmol DMSO n/a 1 mL Total 1 mL Mix well by pipetting. Dispense to 10 μL each. The solution can be stored at -80 °C for 1 year.

steps.title: GO solution Reagent Final concentration Volume...
steps.instruction: GO solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 1 mL) Glucose oxidase 1 mg/mL 1 mg PB (pH 7.
0.2 M 0.1 M 500 μL ddH 2 O n/a up to 1 mL Total 1 mL Mix well by pipetting. Dispense to 50 μL each. The solution can be stored at -80 °C for 1 year.

steps.title: 2% BSA in 0
steps.instruction: 2% BSA in 0.1 M PB Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 50 mL) BSA 2% (w/v) 1 g PB (pH 7.
0.2 M 0.1 M 25 mL ddH 2 O n/a up to 50 mL Total 50 mL Mix well by stirring. Sterilize the solution with a 0.22 μm filter. The solution can be stored at 4 °C for 3 months.

steps.title: FT-GO reaction mixture Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: FT-GO reaction mixture Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 1 mL) CF tyramide solution 10 μM 5 μL GO solution 3 μg/mL 3 μL 2% BSA in 0.1 M PB n/a 992 μL Total 1 mL Mix well by pipetting. The solution should be prepared before use.

steps.title: β-D-glucose solution Reagent Final concentration Volume...
steps.instruction: β-D-glucose solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 1 mL) β-D-glucose 200 mg/mL 200 mg ddH 2 O n/a up to 1 mL Total 1 mL Mix well by pipetting. Dispense to 50 μL each. The solution can be stored at -80 °C for 1 year.

steps.title: Sca l eS4 solution Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: Sca l eS4 solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 100 mL) Urea 4 M 24.02 g D-sorbitol 40% (w/v) 40 g Glycerol 10% (w/v) 10 g Triton-X100 10% (w/v) 0.2% (w/v) 2 mL DMSO 25% (v/v) 25 mL ddH 2 O n/a up to 100 mL Total 100 mL For a detailed procedure for preparation of this solution, refer to Miyawaki et al. [15]. The solution can be stored at 4 °C for 1 month.

steps.title: solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for...
steps.instruction: solution Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 100 mL) Urea 4 M 24.02 g D-sorbitol 40% (w/v) 40 g Glycerol 10% (w/v) 10 g DMSO 25% (v/v) 25 mL ddH 2 O n/a up to 100 mL Total 100 mL For a detailed procedure for preparation of this solution, refer to Miyawaki et al. [15]. The solution can be stored at 4 °C for 1 month.

steps.title: Sca l eS4 gel Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: Sca l eS4 gel Reagent Final concentration Volume (for 100 mL) Agarose 1.5% (w/v) 1.5 g Sca l eS4 D25(

steps.title: n/a up to 100 mL Total...
steps.instruction: n/a up to 100 mL Total 100 mL For a detailed procedure for preparation of this solution, refer to Miyawaki et al. [15]. The solution can be stored at 4 °C for 1 year. Sca l eS4 gel solidifies at 4 °C. Remelt the gel by heating it in a microwave before use. Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Filter paper No
steps.instruction: Filter paper No. 2 (ADVANTEC, catalog number:

steps.title: Custom-made imaging chambers [16]; three-dimensional computer-aided...
steps.instruction: Custom-made imaging chambers [16]; three-dimensional computer-aided design (3D CAD) data for the imaging chamber are provided in Furuta et al. [16]

steps.title: Plate 293T cells on a 6-well...
steps.instruction: Plate 293T cells on a 6-well plate in 2 mL per well of 293T cell culture medium so they will be 70%–90% confluent on the day of transfection.

steps.title: Prepare Lipofectamine 3000 solution, POD-nAb plasmid...
steps.instruction: Prepare Lipofectamine 3000 solution, POD-nAb plasmid DNA with P3000 solution, and then plasmid DNA–lipid complex (see Recipes 2–4).

steps.title: Add 250 μL per well of...
steps.instruction: Add 250 μL per well of the plasmid DNA–lipid complex to the 293T cells.

steps.title: Incubate the cells for 3 days...
steps.instruction: Incubate the cells for 3 days at 37 °C in a CO 2 incubator (5% CO 2 , 95% humidity).

steps.title: Collect the culture supernatant into a 50 mL conical tube
steps.instruction: Collect the culture supernatant into a 50 mL conical tube.

steps.title: Remove cell debris by centrifugation at...
steps.instruction: Remove cell debris by centrifugation at 3,000× g for 5 min at 4 °C. Filter the culture supernatant through a 0.22 μm syringe filter. Add thimerosal 10% into the POD-nAb culture supernatant at the final concentration of 0.05% and store at 4 °C. The POD-nAb culture supernatant can be stored at 4 °C for 6 months and used for immunolabeling. Caution: Thimerosal is an alkylmercury compound. Avoid inhalation or contact with skin, eyes, and mucous membranes. Handle it inside a fume hood with appropriate protective gear. B. AAV vector injection Caution: Biosafety should be considered with respect to the precise nature of experiments being performed. AAV vectors can be handled in BSL-1 facilities in most cases.

steps.title: Anesthetize an adult mouse (8–16 weeks old) by isoflurane inhalation
steps.instruction: Anesthetize an adult mouse (8–16 weeks old) by isoflurane inhalation.

steps.title: Inject 100 μL of an AAV...
steps.instruction: Inject 100 μL of an AAV solution into the retro-orbital sinus of the mouse using a 30-G insulin syringe. Critical: The optimal concentration of the AAV-GFP vector should be determined for each experiment.

steps.title: Return the mouse to its home...
steps.instruction: Return the mouse to its home cage with ad libitum access to food and water. House the mouse in specific pathogen-free conditions for 3–4 weeks. Monitor the health and behavior of the mouse at least twice a week. C. Tissue preparation Following perfusion fixation and post-fixation, brain tissues infected with the AAV-GFP vector are cut into 1-mm-thick brain slices using a vibratome. Preparation of agar blocks for tissue slicing with a vibratome is shown in Figure 1 . Caution: PFA is toxic and teratogenic. Avoid inhalation or contact with skin, eyes, and mucous membranes. Perform steps C1–6 inside a fume hood with appropriate protective gear to limit the exposure to PFA.

steps.title: Filter PBS and fixative solution through...
steps.instruction: Filter PBS and fixative solution through filter paper and chill on ice.

steps.title: Anesthetize the mouse with an intraperitoneal...
steps.instruction: Anesthetize the mouse with an intraperitoneal injection of sodium pentobarbital solution (200 mg/kg). Assess the depth of anesthesia by eye-blink reflexes and toe-pinch withdrawal.

steps.title: Open the thoracic cavity using surgical...
steps.instruction: Open the thoracic cavity using surgical scissors, cut the right atrial appendage, and insert a 23-G needle into the left ventricle of the heart from the apex.

steps.title: Perfuse with 20 mL of ice-cold...
steps.instruction: Perfuse with 20 mL of ice-cold PBS using a 20 mL syringe over 2–3 min.

steps.title: Perfuse with 20 mL of the...
steps.instruction: Perfuse with 20 mL of the ice-cold fixative with another 20 mL syringe over 2–3 min.

steps.title: Excise the brain from the skull...
steps.instruction: Excise the brain from the skull using surgical scissors and tweezers.

steps.title: Transfer the brain in 12 mL...
steps.instruction: Transfer the brain in 12 mL of fixative solution in a 15 mL conical tube and gently shake (50–100 rpm) for 24 h at 4 °C. Pause point: Brain tissues can be stored in 0.02% NaN 3 in PBS at 4 °C for 4 weeks. Brain tissues should be washed extensively with PBS after storage to remove NaN 3 .

steps.title: Pour 10 mL of 4% agar...
steps.instruction: Pour 10 mL of 4% agar in PBS into a well of a 6-well plate.

steps.title: Submerge the brain tissue in the...
steps.instruction: Submerge the brain tissue in the agar solution and leave the plate on ice until the agar solidifies. Note: The temperature of the agar solution should be between 40 and 45 °C.

steps.title: Remove the embedded brain tissue and...
steps.instruction: Remove the embedded brain tissue and agar from the well and trim excess agar around the brain tissue to form a cuboid shape using a razor blade. Keep the brain tissue in the middle of the agar block.

steps.title: Glue the agar block to the...
steps.instruction: Glue the agar block to the bottom of a specimen tray with super glue and pour PB (pH 7.

steps.title: Cut 1-mm-thick brain slices using a...
steps.instruction: Cut 1-mm-thick brain slices using a vibratome and collect the slices in 8 mL of PBS in a well of a 6-well plate. Pause point: Brain slices can be stored in 0.02% NaN 3 in PBS at 4 °C for 4 weeks. Brain slices should be washed extensively with PBS after storage to remove NaN 3 . Figure

steps.title: Preparation of agar blocks for tissue slicing with a vibratome
steps.instruction: Preparation of agar blocks for tissue slicing with a vibratome. An embedded brain tissue and agar are removed from a well of a 6-well plate. Excess agar around the brain tissue is trimmed away to form a cuboid shape using a razor blade. The brain tissue should be kept in the middle of the agar block. The cuboid agar block is mounted onto the bottom of a specimen tray with super glue. D. 3D-immunolabeling with POD-nAb/FT-GO Critical: Brain slices should be shaken thoroughly in solutions during the staining procedure. This is critical for staining homogeneity and the signal/noise ratio in POD-nAb/FT-GO 3D-IHC. Critical: From step D8 onward, brain slices should be protected from light to avoid fading of the fluorescent dye.

steps.title: Transfer 2–3 brain slices with a...
steps.instruction: Transfer 2–3 brain slices with a spatula into 8 mL of prewarmed Sca l eA2 solution in a 6-well cell culture plate and incubate for 24 h at 37 °C with shaking (90 rpm) on a constant-temperature incubator shaker.

steps.title: Wash the brain slices with 8...
steps.instruction: Wash the brain slices with 8 mL of PBS in a 6-well cell culture plate for 15 min twice on a rotary shaker at 20–25 °C with gentle rocking (50–100 rpm). Pause point: Brain slices can be stored in 0.02% NaN 3 in PBS at 4 °C for 1 week. Brain slices should be washed extensively with PBS after storage to remove NaN 3 .

steps.title: Transfer the brain slices with a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the brain slices with a spatula into 6 mL of endogenous peroxidase quenching solution in a 6-well cell culture plate and incubate for 1 h on the rotary shaker at 20–25 °C with gentle rocking (50–100 rpm). Caution: H 2 O 2 in the endogenous peroxidase quenching solution is corrosive and can cause local tissue damage. Avoid inhalation or contact with skin, eyes, and mucous membranes. Handle the quenching solution inside a fume hood with appropriate protective gear. Critical: Inadequate blocking of endogenous peroxidase leads to false-positive signals.

steps.title: Wash the brain slices with 8...
steps.instruction: Wash the brain slices with 8 mL of PBS in a 6-well cell culture plate for 15 min twice on the rotary shaker at 20–25 °C with gentle rocking (50–100 rpm).

steps.title: Transfer the brain slices into 1,200...
steps.instruction: Transfer the brain slices into 1,200 μL of POD-nAb solution in a 2 mL safe-lock tube with a spatula. Incubate the brain slices for 20–24 h on the rotary shaker at 20–25 °C with gentle rocking (50–100 rpm).

steps.title: Wash the brain slices with PBS-X...
steps.instruction: Wash the brain slices with PBS-X in a 6-well cell culture plate for 30 min four times on the rotary shaker at 20–25 °C with gentle rocking (50–100 rpm).

steps.title: Wash the brain slices with PB (pH 7
steps.instruction: Wash the brain slices with PB (pH 7.
0.1 M in a 6-well cell culture plate for 5 min three times on the rotary shaker at 20–25 °C with gentle rocking (50–100 rpm).

steps.title: Transfer the brain slices into 1,200...
steps.instruction: Transfer the brain slices into 1,200 μL of FT-GO reaction mixture in a 2 mL safe-lock tube with a spatula and incubate the brain slices for 4 h on the rotary shaker at 20–25 °C with gentle rocking (50–100 rpm).

steps.title: Add 12 μL of β-D-glucose solution...
steps.instruction: Add 12 μL of β-D-glucose solution into the FT-GO reaction mixture and incubate the brain slices for 30–120 min on the rotary shaker at 20–25 °C with gentle rocking (50–100 rpm). Critical: The optimal duration of the FT-GO reaction should be determined for each experiment. The optimal duration can be determined by whether signal enhancement has reached saturation.

steps.title: Wash the brain slices with PBS-X...
steps.instruction: Wash the brain slices with PBS-X in a 6-well cell culture plate for 15 min twice on the rotary shaker at 20–25 °C with gentle rocking (50–100 rpm).

steps.title: With a spatula, transfer the brain...
steps.instruction: With a spatula, transfer the brain slices into 6 mL of fixative solution in a 6-well cell culture plate. Incubate for 20–24 h on the rotary shaker at 4 °C with gentle rocking (50–100 rpm). Caution: PFA in the fixative solution is toxic and teratogenic. Avoid inhalation or contact with skin, eyes, and mucous membranes. Handle the solution inside a fume hood with appropriate protective gear.

steps.title: Wash the brain slices with 8...
steps.instruction: Wash the brain slices with 8 mL of PBS in a 6-well cell culture plate for 15 min twice on the rotary shaker at 20–25 °C with gentle rocking (50–100 rpm). Pause point: Brain slices can be stored in 0.02% NaN 3 in PBS at 4 °C for 4 weeks. E. Tissue clearing and CLSM imaging Note: We use a customizable 3D-printed imaging chamber for mounting cleared brain slices ( Figure 2 ). 3D CAD data for the imaging chamber are provided in Furuta et al. [16]. Detailed procedures for tissue mounting onto the imaging chamber are also described in Yamauchi et al. [17,18]. Figure

steps.title: Mounting brain slices on the imaging chamber
steps.instruction: Mounting brain slices on the imaging chamber. Customizable 3D-printed imaging chambers are used for mounting of cleared brain slices. 3D CAD data for the imaging chamber are provided in Furuta et al. [16]. The imaging chamber is composed of a chamber frame and a bottom coverslip [16]. The procedure for mounting brain slices is as follows:

steps.title: mount cleared brain slices on the...
steps.instruction: mount cleared brain slices on the bottom coverslip of an imaging chamber;

steps.title: drop Sca l eS4 gel on...
steps.instruction: drop Sca l eS4 gel on the cleared brain slice to fill the imaging chamber;

steps.title: mount a coverslip on the imaging...
steps.instruction: mount a coverslip on the imaging chamber filled with Sca l eS4 gel;

steps.title: place a piece of Kimwipe paper on the coverslip;
steps.instruction: place a piece of Kimwipe paper on the coverslip;

steps.title: place a glass slide on top of the paper; and
steps.instruction: place a glass slide on top of the paper; and

steps.title: place metal weights on the slide...
steps.instruction: place metal weights on the slide glass in a refrigerator at 4 °C. Excessive Sca l eS4 solution around the cleared brain slices should be removed before dropping Sca l eS4 gel.

steps.title: Transfer the brain slices with a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the brain slices with a spatula into 8 mL of prewarmed Sca l eS4 solution in a 6-well cell culture plate and incubate for 12–16 h at 37 °C with shaking (90 rpm) on the constant-temperature incubator shaker. The brain slices become transparent after incubation in Sca l eS4 solution.

steps.title: Place a cleared brain slice on...
steps.instruction: Place a cleared brain slice on the bottom coverslip of an imaging chamber.

steps.title: Remove excessive Sca l eS4 solution...
steps.instruction: Remove excessive Sca l eS4 solution around the cleared brain slice.

steps.title: Drop 600–800 μL of Sca l...
steps.instruction: Drop 600–800 μL of Sca l eS4 gel on the cleared slice to fill the imaging chamber.

steps.title: Mount a coverslip on the imaging...
steps.instruction: Mount a coverslip on the imaging chamber filled with Sca l eS4 gel. Place a piece of Kimwipe paper on the coverslip and then place a glass slide on top of the paper.

steps.title: Transfer the imaging chamber to a refrigerator at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Transfer the imaging chamber to a refrigerator at 4 °C.

steps.title: Place metal weights on the slide...
steps.instruction: Place metal weights on the slide glass and allow Sca l eS4 gel to solidify for 30–60 min.

steps.title: Remove the metal weights, slide glass,...
steps.instruction: Remove the metal weights, slide glass, and Kimwipe paper from the imaging chamber and wipe away excess Sca l eS4 gel.

steps.title: Attach the imaging chamber to the...
steps.instruction: Attach the imaging chamber to the bottom of a 60-mm glass Petri dish with Blu-Tack ® . Adhesion at multiple points is required. For a detailed procedure for attachment of the imaging chamber, refer to Yamauchi et al. [17,18].

steps.title: Pour Sca l eS4 solution into...
steps.instruction: Pour Sca l eS4 solution into the dish and incubate the imaging chamber containing the cleared brain slice in the solution for 30–60 min.

steps.title: Set the correction collar of a...
steps.instruction: Set the correction collar of a 16× multi-immersion objective lens to 1.

steps.title: Critical
steps.instruction: Critical: Refractive index (RI) mismatch-induced aberrations can disturb image formation [17]. The Sca l eS4 solution has an RI of approximately 1.47 [15,19].

steps.title: Mount the 60-mm glass Petri dish...
steps.instruction: Mount the 60-mm glass Petri dish containing the imaging chamber on a microscope stage.

steps.title: Immerse the objective lens in Sca...
steps.instruction: Immerse the objective lens in Sca l eS4 solution and make it slowly approach the cleared brain slice. Critical: Air bubbles trapped on the tip of the objective lens should be removed.

steps.title: Adjust imaging acquisition settings
steps.instruction: Adjust imaging acquisition settings. These include laser power, scan speed, pinhole diameter, detector gain, amplifier offset/gain, xy and z -axis resolution, and bit intensity resolution. Images should be acquired using a z-compensation mode in which the laser intensity and/or detection gain increase with imaging depth to compensate for depth-dependent signal loss.

steps.title: Collect images by CLSM and save the acquired images
steps.instruction: Collect images by CLSM and save the acquired images.

steps.title: Image processing is done with LAS-X and/or Fiji software
steps.instruction: Image processing is done with LAS-X and/or Fiji software.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol or parts of it have been used and validated in the following research article: • Yamauchi et al. [14]. A three dimensional immunolabeling method with peroxidase-fused nanobodies and fluorochromized tyramide-glucose oxidase signal amplification. Commun Biol (Figures 1c, 5b–c, and 8c–d, and Supplementary Movies 1 and 3). We provide validation data in Figure 3, which shows an example of GFP POD-nAb/FT-GO 3D-IHC in the cerebral cortex of a 1-mm-thick brain slice infected with AAV2/PHP.eB CAG EGFP WPRE (n = 3 slices from 3 mice). xy images obtained at depths of 100, 500, and 900 μm are represented in each panel (Figure 3A–C). Neuronal and glial processes in a 1-mm-thick mouse brain slice are stained with a high signal/noise ratio by POD-nAb/FT-GO 3D-IHC for GFP. The staining procedure of GFP POD-nAb/3D-IHC can be completed within three days. POD-nAb/FT-GO 3D-IHC allows for highly sensitive detection of target molecules in mouse brain tissues. We have shown that POD-nAb/FT-GO for GFP yields 9.0- and 6.8-fold signal amplification compared with EGFP fluorescence and conventional 3D-IHC using a synthetic fluorophore-conjugated anti-GFP nAb, respectively [14]. Figure 3. GFP POD-nAb/FT-GO 3D-IHC in a 1-mm-thick mouse brain slice. (A–C) xy images at depths of 100, 500, and 900 μm in a 1-mm-thick brain slice stained by POD-nAb/FT-GO 3D-IHC for GFP. The brain slice is prepared from a mouse infected with AAV2/PHP.eB CAG-EGFP-WPRE. The images are acquired with z-compensation to compensate for depth-dependent signal loss. The pinhole size is set to 1.0 Airy unit during imaging acquisition. Scale bar: 50 μm. POD-nAb, peroxidase-fused nanobody; FT-GO, fluorochromized tyramide-glucose oxidase; 3D-IHC, three-dimensional immunohistochemistry; GFP, green fluorescent protein.
